title: Off-by-one string slicing after device-prefix length change (CUDA:→NPU:)
symptom: After Phase 4 rule migration replaces startswith('cuda:') with startswith('npu:'), adjacent code using v[5:] to extract device indices fails silently — extracting ':1' instead of '1' from 'npu:1', causing validation to reject valid NPU device identifiers with no import errors or tracebacks.
root_cause: Phase 4 rules handle string patterns but NOT integer literals in adjacent slice operations. When 'cuda:' (5 chars) → 'npu:' (4 chars), the integer 5 in v[5:] encoding the old prefix length is not updated because integer literals are not string patterns. The relationship is operational, not lexical.
fix_steps: 1. After Phase 4 rule migration, grep for startswith('npu:') patterns in all migrated files.
2. Within 5 lines of each match, look for string slicing of form VAR[N:] where N is a literal integer.
3. Check if N equals the old CUDA prefix length: 'cuda:' → 5, 'cuda' → 4 — if yes, the index needs updating.
4. Update N to the new NPU prefix length: 'npu:' → 4, 'npu' → 3.
5. Re-run device validation tests with NPU identifiers ('npu:0', 'npu:1', 'npu:10') to confirm correctness.
affected_patterns: v.startswith('npu:') with adjacent v[N:] where N >= 4
_validate_device-style validators extracting index by prefix slicing
Code using if cond: + index = value[N:] on device identifier strings

code_changes.description: v[5:] → v[4:] in _validate_device — 'npu:' is 4 chars, not 5 like 'cuda:'

references: phase_4_rule_migration.json: 25 device_string and 20 device_index replacements — neither handles adjacent slice indices
phase_5_validation_final.json: iteration_count=1, error category=code_adapter, v[5:]→v[4:] confirmed
antipatterns: Do NOT blindly search-and-replace all v[5:] → v[4:] — only fix slices near device prefix checks.
Do NOT skip re-running tests after the fix — this bug produces silent validation failures.
Do NOT assume Phase 4 rules catch all CUDA→NPU issues — integer literals encoding prefix lengths are invisible to pattern matching.

meta.refiner_reasoning: Single candidate from this run. The off-by-one pattern is a systematic blind spot in Phase 4 rule-based migration. Grounded in config.py:242 fix (v[5:]→v[4:]), confirmed by 135/157 unit tests passing. Confidence 0.85: high generalizability to any CUDA→NPU project using string-slicing device extraction, reduced slightly because not all projects use this idiom.
